label: Financial Model
description: Spec a driver-based financial model with assumptions and scenarios

fields.label: Company / project
fields.placeholder: Acme Analytics

fields.label: Business model & revenue logic
fields.placeholder: B2B SaaS, monthly subscriptions, two tiers, sales-led with a free trial.

fields.label: Currency
fields.placeholder: USD

fields.label: Known assumptions / constraints
fields.placeholder: Starting ARR $1.2M, 4% monthly churn, 18-month CAC payback target.

template:
You are a finance professional who builds investor-grade financial models. Design a {{modelType}} for {{company}}{{#if currency}} in {{currency}}{{/if}}.

## Business model
{{businessModel}}

## Model design
- Forecast horizon: {{horizon}}, monthly granularity rolling up to annual.
{{#if drivers}}- Model these drivers explicitly with their own assumption inputs: {{drivers}}.
{{/if}}- Separate clearly: assumptions/inputs, calculations, and outputs. No hardcoded values inside formulas.
{{#if scenarios}}- Build base, bull, and bear scenarios driven by a single switch.
{{/if}}{{#if constraints}}- Anchor to these known assumptions: {{constraints}}.
{{/if}}
## What to produce
- The full tab/section structure and the line items in each.
- The formula logic for every driver, stated in plain terms.
- Key output metrics and a sensitivity table on the 2-3 most important drivers.

## Deliverables
1. The model structure and assumption schema.
2. Worked calculations for a representative period and the headline output summary.
3. A list of assumptions to validate and their biggest risks.

State every assumption explicitly. Proceed with reasonable, clearly-labeled defaults where inputs are missing; ask only if a number is genuinely blocking.

label: Budget Plan
description: Build an annual or departmental budget with assumptions and phasing

fields.label: Budget scope
fields.placeholder: FY2027 Marketing department budget

fields.label: Context & objectives
fields.placeholder: Aggressive growth year; targeting 40% pipeline increase; headcount up from 8 to 12.

fields.label: Prior period actuals / baseline
fields.placeholder: FY2026 spend: payroll $720k, tools $90k, programs $310k.

template:
You are an FP&A lead. Build a {{period}} budget for {{scope}} using a {{method}} approach.

## Objectives & context
{{context}}

## Budget construction
{{#if categories}}- Build line items grouped by: {{categories}}.
{{/if}}{{#if priorActuals}}- Baseline against prior actuals: {{priorActuals}}.
{{/if}}- For each line: the amount, the assumption/driver behind it, and a one-line justification.
{{#if phasing}}- Phase the budget month-by-month to reflect hiring ramps, seasonality, and program timing.
{{/if}}- Tie spend back to the stated objectives; flag any line not clearly serving a goal.

## Deliverables
1. The full budget table by category and line item with totals.
2. The assumptions register driving the numbers.
3. Top risks and 2-3 contingency levers if a 10% cut is required.

Make explicit, conservative assumptions and label them. Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if a figure is genuinely blocking.

label: Variance Analysis
description: Analyze actuals vs budget and explain the drivers behind variances

fields.label: Budget vs actuals data
fields.placeholder: Revenue: budget $2.4M, actual $2.1M. Opex: budget $1.8M, actual $1.95M. ...

fields.label: Materiality threshold
fields.placeholder: Explain variances over 5% or $25k

fields.label: Known context for variances
fields.placeholder: A large deal slipped to Q2; one-off legal spend on the Beta acquisition.

template:
You are a financial controller presenting a variance analysis for {{period}}, viewed {{lens}}.

## Data
{{data}}

## Analysis instructions
{{#if threshold}}- Focus on material variances: {{threshold}}.
{{/if}}- For each material variance: state actual vs budget, the absolute and % variance, and whether it is favorable or unfavorable.
- Explain the most likely root cause; distinguish one-offs from structural/recurring shifts.
{{#if decomposition}}- Where relevant, decompose variance into price, volume, and mix effects.
{{/if}}{{#if knownReasons}}- Incorporate this known context where it explains the numbers: {{knownReasons}}.
{{/if}}{{#if forecastImpact}}- Assess the implication for the full-year forecast and whether a reforecast is warranted.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. An executive summary: the 3-5 variances that actually matter and why.
2. A line-by-line variance table with explanations.
3. Recommended actions and any items needing management attention.

Be specific and quantitative; do not over-explain immaterial noise. Flag where data is insufficient rather than guessing the cause.

label: Board Report
description: Draft a concise, decision-oriented financial report for the board

fields.label: Company
fields.placeholder: Acme Analytics

fields.label: Performance summary & metrics
fields.placeholder: ARR $14.2M (+6% QoQ), net retention 112%, cash $9M, runway 16 months.

fields.label: Key wins & lowlights
fields.placeholder: Closed two enterprise logos; churn ticked up in SMB; hiring behind plan.

fields.label: Decisions or approvals requested
fields.placeholder: Approve $1.5M increase to the FY marketing budget; ratify new option pool.

template:
You are a CFO preparing a board report for {{company}} covering {{period}}. Write in a {{tone}}, decision-oriented style.

## Performance
{{performance}}

{{#if highlights}}## Wins & lowlights
{{highlights}}

{{/if}}## Report structure
{{#if sections}}- Include these sections: {{sections}}.
{{/if}}- Lead with a one-paragraph executive summary stating whether the company is on, ahead of, or behind plan.
- Present financials against plan with concise commentary on material variances only.
{{#if asks}}- Be explicit about decisions requested: {{asks}}. State the recommendation and the implication of each.
{{/if}}- Be candid about risks; pair every risk with a mitigation and owner.

## Deliverables
1. The full board report, skimmable, with headline metrics surfaced first.
2. A clear "asks of the board" section with recommended decisions.

Be concise and honest; no spin. Use the numbers given; flag any gap as a follow-up rather than estimating.

label: Pricing Analysis
description: Analyze and recommend a pricing and packaging strategy

fields.label: Product / offering
fields.placeholder: Acme Analytics Pro plan

fields.label: Current pricing & context
fields.placeholder: Flat $99/user/mo; complaints it's pricey for small teams; competitor at $79.

fields.label: Customer segments
fields.placeholder: SMB, mid-market, enterprise

fields.label: Competitor pricing
fields.placeholder: Competitor A $79/user; Competitor B usage-based ~$0.02/event.

template:
You are a pricing strategist. Analyze pricing for {{product}} with the primary objective to {{objective}}.

## Current state
{{current}}
{{#if segments}}- Customer segments: {{segments}}.
{{/if}}{{#if competitors}}
## Competitive pricing
{{competitors}}
{{/if}}
## Analysis
{{#if model}}- Evaluate these pricing models against the objective: {{model}}. State pros, cons, and revenue/risk implications of each.
{{/if}}{{#if elasticity}}- Reason about willingness-to-pay and price elasticity per segment, including expected behavioral effects of a change.
{{/if}}- Recommend a value metric and packaging that aligns price with realized customer value.
- Quantify the likely impact of the recommendation with stated assumptions, including downside risk.

## Deliverables
1. A clear recommendation: model, packaging, and price points by segment.
2. The reasoning and the assumptions behind the numbers.
3. A migration plan for existing customers and a metric set to validate post-launch.

Be explicit about assumptions and quantify where possible. Proceed with reasoned defaults; flag where market testing is needed rather than asserting certainty.

label: Expense Policy
description: Draft a clear, enforceable employee expense and reimbursement policy

fields.label: Company name
fields.placeholder: Acme Inc.

fields.label: Company context
fields.placeholder: 120-person remote-first company, US + EU staff, frequent client travel.

fields.label: Specific limits & rules
fields.placeholder: Meals $60/day domestic; economy flights <6h; receipts required >$25.

fields.label: Reimbursement timeline
fields.placeholder: Submit within 30 days; paid in the next payroll cycle

fields.label: Use per-diem instead of itemized meals

template:
You are a finance operations lead drafting an employee expense and reimbursement policy for {{company}}.

## Company context
{{context}}

## Policy scope
{{#if categories}}- Cover these categories with clear, enforceable rules: {{categories}}.
{{/if}}- Approval model: {{approval}}.
{{#if perDiem}}- Use a per-diem allowance for meals rather than itemized receipts; state the rates and how they apply.
{{else}}- Reimburse meals on an itemized, receipt-backed basis within stated limits.
{{/if}}{{#if limits}}- Apply these specific limits and rules: {{limits}}.
{{/if}}{{#if reimbursement}}- Reimbursement process and timeline: {{reimbursement}}.
{{/if}}- Define what is non-reimbursable and the consequences of policy violations.

## Policy requirements
- Plain language, scannable structure, with examples of compliant vs non-compliant expenses.
- Clear submission steps, required documentation, and the escalation path for exceptions.

## Deliverables
1. The complete policy document with numbered sections.
2. A one-page employee quick-reference summary.
3. A short list of figures and approvers the company must confirm.

Use sensible market-standard defaults where amounts are unspecified and label them clearly; ask only if a rule is genuinely blocking.

label: Cash Flow Forecast
description: Build a rolling cash flow forecast with runway and scenario stress tests

fields.label: Company
fields.placeholder: Acme Analytics

fields.label: Cash position & inflow/outflow drivers
fields.placeholder: Cash $4.2M; $1.1M MRR collected net-30; payroll $620k/mo; quarterly tax.

fields.label: Known large or one-off movements
fields.placeholder: $900k Series A tranche in week 6; $250k annual insurance in week 9.

template:
You are a treasury and FP&A specialist. Build a {{horizon}} {{method}} cash flow forecast for {{company}}.

## Cash position & drivers
{{position}}
{{#if lumpy}}
## Known lumpy movements
{{lumpy}}
{{/if}}
## Forecast construction
- Period-by-period opening cash, inflows by source, outflows by category, net movement, and closing cash.
- State the timing assumptions (collection lag, payment terms, payroll dates) explicitly.
{{#if runway}}- Calculate runway and flag every period where cash falls below a stated minimum buffer.
{{/if}}{{#if scenarios}}- Run stress scenarios: {{scenarios}}. Show the cash impact and the earliest cash-risk date in each.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. The base-case cash flow table by period with closing balances.
2. Scenario results and the binding constraint in each.
3. Recommended actions to protect liquidity, ranked by speed and impact.

Make timing assumptions explicit and conservative. Proceed with reasoned defaults where inputs are missing; flag the cash-critical unknowns rather than guessing silently.

label: Unit Economics Analysis
description: CAC, LTV, payback and contribution-margin analysis with insights.

fields.label: Business & model
fields.placeholder: B2B SaaS, $99/mo seat-based, mostly self-serve with some sales-assist.

fields.label: Definition of a 'unit'
fields.placeholder: One paying customer account

fields.label: Known metrics & costs
fields.placeholder: CAC ~$1,200; ARPU $140/mo; gross margin 82%; monthly logo churn 2.5%.

fields.label: Segments to break out
fields.placeholder: Self-serve vs sales-assist; SMB vs mid-market

template:
You are a SaaS finance analyst specializing in unit economics. Analyze the unit economics for this business, with a unit defined as: {{unitDef}}.

## Business
{{business}}

## Inputs
{{inputs}}

## Analysis requirements
- Audience: {{audience}}. Horizon: {{horizon}}.
- Compute and show the math for CAC, ARPU/ARPA, gross margin, contribution margin, CAC payback, churn, and LTV (and LTV:CAC).
- State every assumption explicitly; flag any input that looks inconsistent.
{{#if segments}}- Break results out by: {{segments}}.
{{/if}}{{#if benchmarks}}- Compare key ratios to typical SaaS benchmarks and interpret the gap.
{{/if}}{{#if sensitivity}}- Add a sensitivity view on the 2-3 drivers that move LTV:CAC most.
{{/if}}- End with the single biggest lever to improve unit economics.

## Deliverables
1. A clear results table with formulas shown.
2. 3-5 plain-language insights and the key risk.
3. The recommended next action.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Investor Update
description: Monthly or quarterly investor update that builds trust and asks clearly.

fields.label: Company
fields.placeholder: Northstar Insights

fields.label: Period covered
fields.placeholder: April 2026

fields.label: Key metrics (with prior period)
fields.placeholder: MRR $182k (+9%), net new logos 14, runway 11 mo, cash $1.4M, NRR 112%.

fields.label: Wins
fields.placeholder: Closed first 6-figure deal; shipped SSO; hired Head of Sales.

fields.label: Challenges & lowlights
fields.placeholder: Onboarding time too long; one key churn; pipeline thin for Q3.

fields.label: Specific asks
fields.placeholder: Intros to 2 enterprise CISOs; a VP Eng candidate referral.

template:
You are a founder-CFO writing a credible {{cadence}} investor update for {{company}}, covering {{period}}.

## Metrics
{{metrics}}

## Wins
{{wins}}

## Challenges
{{challenges}}

## Writing rules
- Be transparent and concise; lead with a one-line TL;DR and the top metric.
- Present metrics with trend vs prior period; never bury bad news.
- Frame challenges with what you're doing about each, not excuses.
{{#if includeAsk}}- Make asks specific and actionable: {{#if asks}}{{asks}}{{else}}name concrete, easy-to-act-on requests{{/if}}.
{{/if}}- Keep it scannable: short sections, numbers first, no fluff or spin.

## Deliverables
1. The full investor update email.
2. A 3-bullet TL;DR for the top of the message.
3. A one-line subject line.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Scenario & Sensitivity Analysis
description: Base/upside/downside scenarios with drivers and decision triggers.

fields.label: Decision or question
fields.placeholder: Should we hire 6 reps now given uncertain pipeline and 12-month runway?

fields.label: Baseline assumptions
fields.placeholder: Revenue $4M, growth 30% YoY, gross margin 78%, burn $220k/mo, cash $2.6M.

fields.label: Key uncertain drivers
fields.placeholder: Sales productivity, churn, fundraise timing, CAC inflation.

fields.label: Primary outcome metric
fields.placeholder: Months of runway / EOY cash balance

template:
You are a corporate FP&A lead building decision-grade scenario analysis. Address this decision: {{decision}}

## Baseline assumptions
{{baseline}}

## Key drivers
{{drivers}}

## Analysis requirements
- Audience: {{audience}}. Horizon: {{horizon}}. Primary metric: {{metric}}.
- Model these scenarios: {{scenarios}}. For each, state the driver assumptions and resulting {{metric}}.
- Show the math/logic and keep assumptions explicit and internally consistent.
- Identify which 2-3 drivers the outcome is most sensitive to.
{{#if triggers}}- Define concrete decision triggers / tripwires: what observed signal should change the decision and by when.
{{/if}}- Give a clear recommendation under uncertainty, with the main risk called out.

## Deliverables
1. A scenario comparison table (assumptions vs {{metric}}).
2. Sensitivity ranking of the top drivers.
3. The recommendation plus tripwires to monitor.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.
